title: Dash [Action]
excerpt: The Dash action spends a creature's action to add extra movement equal to its current speed for the turn.
markdown:
# Dash [Action]

The Dash action converts a creature's [[action]] into more movement for the current turn. When a creature Dashes, it gains extra movement equal to its current [[speed]].

## Core Effect

- Dash adds movement for the current turn instead of changing the creature's base speed
- the extra movement is measured from the speed the creature currently has after other active modifiers are applied
- the added movement can be spent in the same turn only; unused movement does not carry forward

## Movement Modes

Dash supports whatever movement modes the creature can currently use. If the creature is moving across the ground, [[climbing|Climbing]], [[swimming|Swimming]], or [[flying|Flying]], the extra movement can be spent through that mode as long as the world state still allows it.

## Limits

- Dash does not remove terrain costs, movement penalties, or speed reductions
- Dash does not prevent hostile reactions, including movement-based triggers
- Dash does not let a creature move through blocked space or ignore other restrictions on movement
